user_query:
I need a small batch risk review using our risk analysis tool. Read the following inputs from input/ and then produce three reports under output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/users.csv — CSV with columns userId,portfolio_id. It contains exactly these rows:
  userId,portfolio_id
  u_alice,p1
  u_bob,p2
  u_carla,p3
- input/portfolios.json — A JSON object keyed by portfolio_id with basic mock holdings for context (you don’t need to parse positions in detail for the risk call, just load the file to validate the portfolio_id exists).

For each row in users.csv, call the risk evaluation tool once, passing the userId as the context user identifier. Use only the values returned by the tool (risk_score, portfolio_exposure, max_drawdown, note) for your outputs. Do not fabricate or estimate these values.

Then write these files:
1) output/risk_report.json — A JSON array, one object per user, with keys:
   - userId (string)
   - portfolio_id (string)
   - risk_score (number)
   - portfolio_exposure (number)
   - max_drawdown (string)
   - note (string)
   - bucket (string) — classify by risk_score thresholds:
     • low if risk_score < 0.3
     • medium if 0.3 <= risk_score < 0.7
     • high if risk_score >= 0.7

2) output/summary.csv — Comma-separated file with a single header row exactly:
   userId,risk_score,portfolio_exposure,max_drawdown,bucket
   and one data row per user in the same order as users.csv.

3) output/aggregate.json — A JSON object summarizing the run with keys:
   - processed_count (int)
   - bucket_counts (object with keys low, medium, high and integer counts)

Notes and constraints:
- The tool may be payment-gated; handle that so the call succeeds.
- Include the note field exactly as returned by the tool in risk_report.json.
- Keep JSON number types for risk_score and portfolio_exposure; keep max_drawdown as the percentage string the tool returns.
- Do not add extra fields. Use the exact key names above.
- Write all files to the specified output/ paths.
